description: Render: the Talos single-node Flux kustomization resolves without missing files or duplicate resource names.
Render: the `alga-core` appliance overlay renders the root chart successfully with enterprise single-node values.
Render: the new PgBouncer chart renders successfully with appliance values.
Render: the workflow-worker appliance overlay renders successfully and points at the PgBouncer service host/port.
Render: the email-service appliance overlay renders successfully and points at the PgBouncer service host/port.
Render: the temporal-worker appliance overlay renders successfully and points at the PgBouncer service host/port.
Render: the appliance profile defines a Temporal release with single-node-safe persistence defaults.
Contract: root-chart DB secret template keeps an existing `db-credentials` secret instead of regenerating passwords on reinstall.
Contract: the root chart no longer emits separate migration and seed hooks when the unified bootstrap hook is enabled.
Contract: the unified bootstrap hook uses the setup image and passes `SETUP_RUN_MIGRATIONS` and `SETUP_RUN_SEEDS` control flags.
Contract: `setup/entrypoint.sh` can read the admin password from environment variables when `/run/secrets/postgres_password` is absent.
Contract: the bootstrap path still checks database seed state before running seeds.
Functional: a fresh install path would create DB/users, run migrations, run seeds once, and then allow the app workload to start.
Functional: a second reconcile against an existing seeded database would skip the seed phase and leave persisted data intact.
Functional: the server deployment uses PgBouncer when the appliance profile enables it while bootstrap continues to talk directly to Postgres.
Functional: appliance bootstrap/deploy scripts target the Talos single-node Flux profile path and fail clearly when required inputs are missing.
Documentation: the design doc captures approved release boundaries, first-run semantics, and validation expectations.
